classObjective: Apply geometric postulates about points, lines, and planes to justify statements.
workedExamples: 12-4, Example 1 — Apply Postulates about Points, Lines, and Planes
warmUp: What postulate states that through any two points there is exactly one line?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: postulates for lines, planes, intersections; using postulates to justify geometric statements
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice applying geometric postulates to justify statements
independentPractice: Practice problems applying postulates about points, lines, and planes to justify conclusions
assessment: Exit ticket: which postulate justifies the statement that exactly one line passes through points A and B?
capReflection: Why are postulates considered the foundational truths in an axiomatic geometry system?
